title: Which Method Creates a Resource?
prompt_md: You're building an endpoint that creates a **new** user from a JSON body the client sends. Which HTTP method should the route use?
explanation_md: POST is the conventional method for creating a new resource where the server assigns its identity (e.g. POST /users). GET only reads and shouldn't carry a body or cause changes. DELETE removes. PUT is used to create-or-replace a resource *at a client-specified URL* (PUT /users/42) — for creating into a collection where the server picks the id, POST is the standard choice.
